raw_data.details: Launched on 1st January 1966, the scheme "Junior Hostel" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides free accommodation and mess facilities to students studying in classes 6th to 8th.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be from the Scheduled Tribes (ST) category. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh. The applicant should be a student of class 6th to 8th. The applicant should be studying in a government or recognized school. The applicant should have passed the previous year's examination.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineOfflineRegistration
Step 1: The applicant should visit the Madhya Pradesh Tribal Affairs & Scheduled Caste Welfare Automation System [MPTAAS portal.](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/mptaas)
Step 2: Click on "New Beneficiary Profile [Registration](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/PMS/Registration/BeneficiaryRegistration/PersonalDetail)."
Step 3: Fill in all the required details. Fields marked with * are mandatory. Complete the form by entering all necessary information.
Step 4: Upload all required documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
Step 5: After completing the form and uploading the documents, click "Submit".
Step 6: After successful payment and submission, you will receive a registration number. Login
Step 1: The applicant should visit the Madhya Pradesh Tribal Affairs & Scheduled Caste Welfare Automation System [MPTAAS portal.](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/mptaas)
Step 2: Enter your user ID, password, and captcha code, then click "Login."
Step 3: Choose the scheme you wish to apply for to avail the benefits.
Step 4: Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Step 5: Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotographAadhaar cardDomicile certificate of Madhya PradeshCaste certificate Previous class passing certificate/marksheetSamagra IDBank account details/Bank PassbookOther necessary documents if required

BenefitsFree Accommodation and Mess Facilities. EligibilityThe applicant should be from the Scheduled Tribes (ST) category. The applicant should be a native of Madhya Pradesh. The applicant should be a student of class 6th to 8th. The applicant should be studying in a government or recognized school. The applicant should have passed the previous year's examination. Application ProcessOnlineOfflineRegistrationStep 1: The applicant should visit the Madhya Pradesh Tribal Affairs & Scheduled Caste Welfare Automation System [MPTAAS portal.](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/mptaas)Step 2: Click on "New Beneficiary Profile [Registration](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/PMS/Registration/BeneficiaryRegistration/PersonalDetail)."Step 3: Fill in all the required details. Fields marked with * are mandatory. Complete the form by entering all necessary information. Step 4: Upload all required documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates. Step 5: After completing the form and uploading the documents, click "Submit". Step 6: After successful payment and submission, you will receive a registration number. LoginStep 1: The applicant should visit the Madhya Pradesh Tribal Affairs & Scheduled Caste Welfare Automation System [MPTAAS portal.](https://www.tribal.mp.gov.in/mptaas)Step 2: Enter your user ID, password, and captcha code, then click "Login."Step 3: Choose the scheme you wish to apply for to avail the benefits. Documents RequiredPassport-size PhotographAadhaar cardDomicile certificate of Madhya PradeshCaste certificate Previous class passing certificate/marksheetSamagra IDBank account details/Bank PassbookOther necessary documents if required Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the Junior Hostel Scheme? It is a scheme providing free accommodation and mess facilities to ST students from classes 6th to 8th in Madhya Pradesh. Who is eligible for the scheme? ST students who are natives of Madhya Pradesh, studying in government/recognized schools, and have passed the previous year's examination. What is the main benefit of the scheme? Free accommodation and mess facilities. Which department implements the scheme? The Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of Madhya Pradesh. Which classes are covered under the scheme? Students from classes 6th to 8th are eligible. Is the scheme applicable to students from other categories? No, the scheme is exclusively for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students. Can students from private schools apply? No, the applicant must be studying in a government or recognized school. Is there any income limit for eligibility? No specific income limit is mentioned for this scheme. Is the scheme available across all districts of Madhya Pradesh? Yes, it is implemented across the state. faqs.question: What is the Junior Hostel Scheme?
faqs.answer: It is a scheme providing free accommodation and mess facilities to ST students from classes 6th to 8th in Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: ST students who are natives of Madhya Pradesh, studying in government/recognized schools, and have passed the previous year's examination.

faqs.question: What is the main benefit of the scheme?
Which department implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Which classes are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Students from classes 6th to 8th are eligible.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable to students from other categories?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is exclusively for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students.

faqs.question: Can students from private schools apply?
faqs.answer: No, the applicant must be studying in a government or recognized school.

faqs.question: Is there any income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: No specific income limit is mentioned for this scheme.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available across all districts of Madhya Pradesh?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is implemented across the state.
